source_paper_title: Near-Optimal Performance of Stochastic Model Predictive Control
context:
Remark 5 in Section 2.2 (page 7) and reiterated in Section 4 “Conclusions and Future Work” (page 13).

Nevertheless, the dynamic regret analysis regarding the distributional robustness property remains an open question. While this topic is beyond the scope of this paper, it presents an intriguing direction for future research.
title: Dynamic regret bounds for SMPC under distributional mismatch (e.g., Wasserstein ambiguity)
background_latex:
- Consider a discrete-time stochastic linear system for stages \(t\in\{0,1,\dots,T\}\): \[x_t = A(\xi_t)x_{t-1} + B(\xi_t)u_{t-1} + d(\xi_t),\] where \(x_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n_x}\), \(u_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n_u}\), and \(\xi_t\) is an exogenous random variable.
- The stage cost is quadratic with random coefficients: \[\ell(x_t,u_t;\xi_t)=\tfrac12\big(x_t^\top Q(\xi_t)x_t + u_t^\top R(\xi_t)u_t\big)-q(\xi_t)^\top x_t-r(\xi_t)^\top u_t.\]
- Let \(\mathsf{P}\) denote the true (unknown) joint distribution of \(\xi_{0:T}:=(\xi_0,\dots,\xi_T)\) on a measurable space, and let \(\widehat{\mathsf{P}}\) denote a nominal distribution used by the controller.
- Fix an initial condition \((\xi_0,w_{-1})\) with \(w_{-1}:=(x_{-1},u_{-1})\). Let \(J_{\mathsf{P}}(\pi;\xi_0,w_{-1})\) be the expected total cost under \(\mathsf{P}\) when applying a nonanticipative policy \(\pi\) (e.g., the optimal policy or an SMPC policy).
- For a prediction horizon \(W\), define the SMPC policy \(\pi^{\mathrm{SMPC}}_{W,\widehat{\mathsf{P}}}\) as the receding-horizon policy that, at each time \(t\), solves the \(W\)-step truncated stochastic control problem using the conditional distribution induced by \(\widehat{\mathsf{P}}\), then applies only the first control.
- Let \(\pi^\star_{\mathsf{P}}\) denote an optimal policy for the full-horizon stochastic control problem under \(\mathsf{P}\).
- Let \(\mathcal{W}(\mathsf{P},\widehat{\mathsf{P}})\) denote the Wasserstein distance between \(\mathsf{P}\) and \(\widehat{\mathsf{P}}\) (with a specified ground metric on the uncertainty space).
problem_definition_latex:
Analyze the dynamic regret of stochastic model predictive control under inexact distribution knowledge. In particular, assume the true distribution \(\mathsf{P}\) is unknown but satisfies a distributional ambiguity constraint \(\mathcal{W}(\mathsf{P},\widehat{\mathsf{P}})\le \varepsilon\) for a given nominal distribution \(\widehat{\mathsf{P}}\) and radius \(\varepsilon\ge 0\). Design conditions and prove an explicit bound (as a function of \(W\), \(T\), and \(\varepsilon\)) on the distributionally robust dynamic regret
\[\sup_{\mathsf{P}:\,\mathcal{W}(\mathsf{P},\widehat{\mathsf{P}})\le \varepsilon}\Big( J_{\mathsf{P}}\big(\pi^{\mathrm{SMPC}}_{W,\widehat{\mathsf{P}}};\xi_0,w_{-1}\big) - J_{\mathsf{P}}\big(\pi^\star_{\mathsf{P}};\xi_0,w_{-1}\big)\Big).\]
Problem: Determine whether this worst-case regret can still decay exponentially in \(W\) (up to an additive term controlled by \(\varepsilon\)), under suitable stabilizability/detectability and boundedness assumptions on \(A(\xi_t),B(\xi_t),Q(\xi_t),R(\xi_t)\).

Analyze the dynamic regret of stochastic model predictive control under inexact distribution knowledge. In particular, assume the true distribution \(\mathsf{P}\) is unknown but satisfies a distributional ambiguity constraint \(\mathcal{W}(\mathsf{P},\widehat{\mathsf{P}})\le \varepsilon\) for a given nominal distribution \(\widehat{\mathsf{P}}\) and radius \(\varepsilon\ge 0\). Problem: Determine whether this worst-case regret can still decay exponentially in \(W\) (up to an additive term controlled by \(\varepsilon\)), under suitable stabilizability/detectability and boundedness assumptions on \(A(\xi_t),B(\xi_t),Q(\xi_t),R(\xi_t)\).
